reasoning:
ACLED measures political violence fatalities (battles, explosions, violence against civilians, riots). The latest known baseline is 1.000, meaning 0 ACLED fatalities in the past 360 days on average. Even updating this to include recent 2024-2025 incidents (which caused single digit deaths), the 30-day average over 360 days remains near 0. Therefore, the formula 1 + (average) yields ~1.000 to ~1.100. For the condition "more than ten times" to be met, 11 to 12 fatalities would need to occur within a 30-day span preceding the resolution dates.

Germany is a stable country where political events leading to >10 fatalities are exceedingly rare (the last one was the 2016 Christmas market attack with 12 fatalities). A month with over 10 fatalities occurs roughly once every decade, or around ~0.8% of the months. For the Nov 30, 2025 resolution date, 23 days of November have already passed with zero known mass-fatality incidents reported. Therefore, it is highly unlikely to resolve YES (p = 0.05, staying slightly above 0 due to minimum limits on confidence). The December 2025 and Feb 2026 windows are similarly extremely unlikely. The probabilities slowly increase slightly reflecting normal horizon uncertainty (p=0.06, p=0.07).
